title: Interesting discovery
wikitext:
[]: Interesting discovery             Quote Post by jacobmm » Thu Jul 10, 2008 4:58 am Hi everyone, When i played the opening of eva with my 2 front speakers disabled(I have 4 speakers in total), it sounds pretty interesting. Well, its something like "hoou hoou ho ho ho" in chorus XP. Its not so amazing when we play it normally and hardly do we notice it. It only works when you enable '2 front 2 rear' as audio device on the player(Stereo wont do). Also your video has to support '2 front 2 rear' mode. Has anyone tried it? (or has this been posted long ago? x.x)

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Jul 10, 2008 7:16 am It's used as part of the opening melody in the Animage Healing version of A Cruel Angel's Thesis - using an equaliser on your computer you can bring different parts of the music from the show's title to the fore.

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Thu Jul 10, 2008 1:43 pm It must be Anno explaining Eva to us as told to him by Freud in a séance. ...just like he did with that Beatles song.

[]:             Quote Post by jacobmm » Thu Jul 10, 2008 3:57 pm Zuggy wrote: It's used as part of the opening melody in the Animage Healing version of A Cruel Angel's Thesis - using an equaliser on your computer you can bring different parts of the music from the show's title to the fore.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jul 10, 2008 6:20 pm jacobmm wrote: Umm... one question, why does surround sound seem more pleasant than stereo or mono? Is the reason psychological or physiological? w00t! Thank you.

[]:             Quote Post by jacobmm » Fri Jul 11, 2008 3:38 am Ornette wrote: If you're watching a movie that has 5.1 sound, then of course it'll sound better with 4+ channels.

[]:             Quote Post by p06 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 6:24 am jacobmm wrote: But why? I mean whether its 2 channel or 4 channel, the sounds are going to the same ear. So why should it be more pleasant?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jul 11, 2008 6:51 am http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gq4xkHNSuYQ Does it sound anything like that? (The video segments are mixed up towards the end, but the audio's all right)

[]:             Quote Post by pata_333 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 12:42 pm THOSE BACKGROUND VOCALS ARE AMAZING!!! can't believe I've never heard them before, i think it is because of the fact that when we are watching the opening, we pay more attention to the image than to the music ;) For example, The Simpsons intro has a really complex song with lots of instruments and percussion, just that we pay more attention to the Image

[]:             Quote Post by jacobmm » Sat Jul 12, 2008 5:13 am THOSE BACKGROUND VOCALS ARE AMAZING!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jul 12, 2008 6:15 am jacobmm wrote: Ornette wrote: If you're watching a movie that has 5.1 sound, then of course it'll sound better with 4+ channels.

[]:             Quote Post by jacobmm » Sat Jul 12, 2008 3:51 pm jacobmm wrote: But why? I mean whether its 2 channel or 4 channel, the sounds are going to the same ear. So why should it be more pleasant? Is it because we are told so then that is what we believe? But the difference is obvious in gaming and such.

It only works when you enable '2 front 2 rear' as audio device on the player(Stereo wont do). Also your video has to support '2 front 2 rear' mode. Has anyone tried it?
